steps.title: Notice both lines pass through the origin
steps.content: Both equations have no constant term: $y=-\frac{1}{5}x$ and $y=\frac{1}{7}x$. That means both lines pass through $(0,0)$, so $x=0$ is already a solution.

steps.title: Confirm algebraically
steps.content: Set the right sides equal: $$-\frac{x}{5}=\frac{x}{7}.$$ Multiplying by $35$ gives $-7x=5x$, so $12x=0$ and $x=0$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is correct. The other choices use nonzero $x$-values, but two different lines through the origin can intersect only at the origin unless they are the same line.
